Original deal: $18,576.72 biennial, closed Jan 12, 2026 by AE Basim Parvizi / SDR Tej Telymonde. Products: Website Design (30 pages) + Advanced Universal AI.

Competitive Phoenix market. Previous AM Tyler got great reviews.

Client did NOT know about directory placements. Then pivoted to a la carte pricing to let him cherry-pick services.
postSaveNotes.resultsMetric: 809 referrals from 9,505 interactions (7.98% conversion), 69% new patients (73% at Biltmore), 660 after-hours referrals from 7,413 after-hours interactions, 64 Healthgrades leads (100% new patients), 39 Missed Call AI leads, 304 blogs (85,297 words), 217 Google reviews, 211 replied, 216 requests sent, 6 negative reviews removed, 459 directory profile views with 50 conversions
postSaveNotes.overallCallRecap: 32-min save call. Dr. Sadowski already committed to EKWA for website/SEO but was genuinely impressed by directory placement data and review scrubbing results he never knew existed. Wants to cherry-pick DearDoc services to complement EKWA. Needs itemized pricing. Also needs website hosting extended month-to-month during EKWA transition. Dobson Ranch location sold to Dr. Payne -- needs separate billing/negotiation. Very amicable -- door wide open.
postSaveNotes.saveTactic: ROI data reveal (directory placements) + a la carte pricing + price protection
postSaveNotes.saveCancelNotes: SAVED -- email sent 3/13 with itemized breakdown. Two options: A ($599/mo) or B ($749/mo). Awaiting reply. Active billing terms consolidated. 2 free months hosting during EKWA transition. Dobson Ranch: stop billing Sadowski, new owner Dr. Payne needs separate outreach. Follow up if no reply by 3/18.

actions.task: Send $97/mo x 6-month offer in writing -- EMAIL SENT 3/16, ACCEPTED (draft reply confirms)
actions.details: COMPLETED 3/16. Email sent at 10:19 PM ET as reply to 'RE: Notice of dispute for DearDoc, Inc payment plan' thread. To: araceli@dietgenics.com, CC: frankv@dietgenics.com. Offer: $97/mo x 6 months (website + chatbot + daily SEO blogs + review management). Original $390/mo x 12-month contract wiped ($2,340 remaining). No auto-renewal. Deadline was March 20th. Draft reply exists confirming Frank and Araceli accepted the restructured agreement. PandaDoc next. Original contract signed 09/22/2025. They have no choice -- contract is enforceable for remaining 8 months at $390/mo, this $97/mo deal is their best path.

postSaveNotes.callParticipants: Stelios Anastasiades, Araceli Vazquez, Frank Vazquez
postSaveNotes.reasonForCancelRequest: Zero ROI -- only 1 patient converted in 4 billing cycles. Website development required excessive client effort (Spanish translation via Google Translate was poor quality, multiple rounds of corrections). Lead quality questioned -- contacts not converting to real patients. 80% Spanish-speaking clientele needs accurate bilingual content.
postSaveNotes.solutionsOffered: 1) Auto-dialer (free add-on) -- declined, doesn't address core need. 2) Voice AI receptionist (free add-on) -- declined, same reason. 3) Financial hardship: $197/mo x 6 months with blogs + upgraded services. 4) Final rock-bottom: $97/mo x 6 months for website + chat + blogs, wiping remaining 8 months of contract balance.
postSaveNotes.solutionsAccepted: ACCEPTED -- $97/mo x 6 months (website + chat + daily SEO blogs + review management). Original contract balance wiped. Frank and Araceli confirmed acceptance via email reply.
postSaveNotes.resultStrategyDiscussed: Path A (ROI/Feature) -- showed dashboard data, after-hours capture, directory placements (Healthgrades, WebMD, ShareCare, Vitals in Richardson TX). Pitched auto-dialer for lead speed-to-contact (391% conversion increase stat). Pitched Voice AI for phone coverage. Both declined -- Frank said 'none of this gets us more patients.' Pivoted to Path C (Financial Hardship) -- $197/mo then $97/mo rock bottom. SEO blog demo with dummy Sedona dentist site. Frank cut off presentation at 44 min mark.
postSaveNotes.resultsMetric: 869 questions answered, 22 referrals (2.5% conversion), 13 new patients / 9 existing, 679 after-hours questions, 13 after-hours referrals, 54 reviews, 30 replied, 5 review requests, avg 2.5 min engagement time, ~1,600 unique visitors, ~50% question rate
postSaveNotes.overallCallRecap: 56-min save call with Araceli Vazquez (dietician/owner) and Frank Vazquez (business partner). Practice serves ~80% Spanish-speaking clients in Richardson TX. Firm cancellation request -- core issue is zero ROI (1 patient in 4 months). Website development was painful (Google Translate for Spanish was terrible, multiple revision rounds). They praised chatbot and website aesthetics. Contract dispute -- Frank says Chase (sales rep) promised cancel anytime after 60 days, disputes 10-day window in TOS. Got confrontational: 'we're not gonna pay anymore' and mentioned recourses. Stelios offered free auto-dialer, free Voice AI -- both declined. Final offer: $97/mo x 6 months (website + chat + blogs), wiping remaining balance. They want it in writing. Tone shifted to cautiously open at the end.
postSaveNotes.saveTactic: Rock-bottom pricing ($97/mo) + blog upgrade + contract balance wipe
postSaveNotes.saveCancelNotes: SAVED. Email sent 3/16 at 10:19 PM ET. Frank and Araceli accepted via reply. $97/mo x 6 months for website + chat + daily SEO blogs + review management. Original $390/mo contract ($2,340 remaining) wiped clean. No auto-renewal -- they decide at end of 6 months. PandaDoc agreement next. Original contract signed 09/22/2025, enforceable through September 2026. This was their best option given contractual obligations.

postSaveNotes.reasonForCancelRequest: Not seeing enough movement in new patient notifications after relaunch. Product is good but too expensive ($500/mo) for her micro business -- affecting her bottom line. Also wanted the chatbot removed from her website entirely.
postSaveNotes.solutionsOffered: 1) Showed 20,000 chatbot interactions all after-hours -- referrals appeared low because system was routing to JNAP booking rather than collecting lead forms, 2) Offered to prorate existing free months (built into old deal through May 2027) into a shorter 6-month term at $197/mo, voiding old agreement, 3) Non-auto-renewing -- at end of term, she tells web developer to remove 4-5 lines of embed code
postSaveNotes.solutionsAccepted: $197/mo x 6 months, non-auto-renewing, ends Sept 17 2026. Signed via payment link on the call.
postSaveNotes.resultStrategyDiscussed: Showed chatbot was actively routing 20,000 interactions to JNAP booking system after-hours. Patient count stable (not growing but being supported by after-hours capture). Low referral count explained by redirect mode -- patients going directly to JNAP instead of submitting lead form.
postSaveNotes.resultsMetric: 20,000 total chatbot interactions, all after-hours, routing to JNAP. Patient count year-over-year stable (compared Jan 2025 to Jan 2026 with Jude -- same numbers). Referrals low due to JNAP redirect mode.
postSaveNotes.overallCallRecap: Christy Contreras wanted to fully cancel and remove the chatbot from her website. She was paying ~$500/mo and felt it was too expensive for a micro business. Her old deal had 6 free months baked in running to May 2027. Stelios showed 20,000 after-hours interactions routing to her JNAP booking system. Offered to prorate the free months into a new 6-month deal at $197/mo -- no auto-renewal, clear exit at Sept 17 2026. She accepted and signed via payment link on the call. Confirmation + cell number email to be sent today. At end of term, she'll have web developer remove chatbot embed code.

Old deal with free months (baked in to May 2027) voided and replaced. Signed via payment link on the call. Products: chatbot with JNAP integration, existing package. At end of term she will remove embed code via web developer. SET CALENDAR REMINDER: early September 2026 to reach out before term ends.

CC Robin on the support ticket for the Google review fix.

postSaveNotes.callParticipants: Stelios Anastasiades, Robin Harrell (Office Manager), Dr. FLV (Practice Owner) -- Frontline Vision
postSaveNotes.reasonForCancelRequest: Lack of results -- not seeing enough new patients. Google auto-review replies stopped working with no explanation. Support turnaround was 3 weeks per issue. ROI not justifying $531/mo. Robin and Dr. were also exploring a competing phone/EHR integration system.
postSaveNotes.solutionsOffered: 1) Showed stats: 1,500 questions, 29 referrals, 1,200+ after-hours sessions, 21 after-hours referrals, 114 blogs, 342 hrs saved, 35,000 SEO keywords, 2) Offered 25% discount ($397/mo) -- declined, 3) Offered $297/mo lowest rate -- hesitant, 4) Offered to remove missed call AI (they have competing phone system), reduce price to $249/mo with all other products kept, 5) Adding video concierge model for free to increase conversions, 6) Fixing Google review connection via support ticket, 7) Stelios providing direct cell + CC on all support tickets
postSaveNotes.solutionsAccepted: $249/mo Financial Hardship rate. Missed call AI removed. Video concierge being added. Google review fix in progress. Stelios direct cell provided.
postSaveNotes.resultStrategyDiscussed: Presented ROI math: 5-10 referrals/mo at $125/visit avg = $625-1,250/mo. Showed after-hours traffic capture at times practice is closed (Jordan at 1:12am example). Demonstrated health grades sponsored profile with appointment click capability. Explained full funnel from site landing to chatbot to JNAP.
postSaveNotes.resultsMetric: 1,500 questions answered, 29 referrals, 1,200+ after-hours sessions, 21 after-hours referrals, 114 blogs, 342 hours saved, ~35,000 SEO keywords. Capturing 1,460 of ~1,800 site visitors. Busiest days: Sunday and Tuesday.
postSaveNotes.overallCallRecap: Robin Harrell (office mgr) and Dr. FLV (owner) joined. Key complaints: not enough new patients for ROI, Google review auto-reply broke and stayed broken for months, support was always 3 weeks out, February stats were poor. They also have a competing phone/EHR system (VoIP) being implemented. Stelios offered discounts: $397 (declined), then $297 (hesitant), then $249 with missed call AI removed since the competing system is taking over phone duties. They accepted $249/mo. Stelios adding video concierge for free, submitting Google review fix ticket, providing direct cell. CC Robin on all support tickets going forward.
postSaveNotes.saveSubReason: Financial Hardship + ROI Concerns + Support Failures
postSaveNotes.saveTactic: Remove conflicting product (missed call AI -- competing system taking over phones), reduce price to $249/mo, add video concierge for free, fix Google review connection, provide direct cell access
postSaveNotes.saveCancelNotes: SAVED. Previous rate $531/mo. New rate $249/mo. CHANGES: (1) missed call AI REMOVED -- they have competing VoIP/EHR phone system taking over, (2) video concierge being ADDED at no charge, (3) Google review auto-reply BROKEN -- submitting support ticket, CC Robin, (4) all other products kept: Universal AI chat, listings (WebMD/Healthgrades/MNT/Sharecare/Vitals), reputation mgmt, SEO blogs, (5) Stelios providing direct cell and handling all support ticket submissions on their behalf. Robin Harrell is office manager. Dr. FLV is owner. Send payment link for updated $249 billing rate. Do NOT schedule a traditional relaunch call -- they want direct cell access and email support only.
